title: If user specifies UIDMapSlice without GIDMapSlice, set them equal
body:
We need to map slices set for both UID and GID maps to be equivalent if
not specified by user.  Currently if you do not specify both the containers
are not running.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
> We need to map slices set for both UID and GID maps to be equivalent if not specified by user.

I'd rather have one or the other being unset be a fatal error in libpod with early checks in the command line and other wrappers.  It's a fairly sensitive issue to be providing implicit fallbacks.  I'd be fine putting this fallback behind and explicit opt-in (e.g. `--gidmap-from-uidmap`).
I'm OK with this behavior as long as it is well-documented in the manpages

Looks like it'll work :+1:.  I'll note that the behavior when we mix one-but-not-both of these options with either-or-both of the options that specify subuid/subgid map entry sets will need to be very clearly documented.
@nalind Should we even allow mixing those flags? I don't think we should allow specifying maps manually if you're already specifying a subuid/subgid map

Pushing a9e9fd4f5b56f0dd91944b86feba9a2d3814d93e to master...

There's no technical reason why they can't be mixed.  The value we use to search `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid` can match multiple entries, which all get used, and adding entries from the command line to the list we get from there (if we look there) seems pretty simple.  We should already get an error at some level if there are overlapping ranges.
@nalind I'm mostly just worried about the complexity and potential confusion it can cause - there are a lot of potentially confusing interactions here.
